login.subtitle: Upload and analyze packet captures with Wireshark-like inspection and AI-powered diagnostics.
login.notAuthorized: Your GitHub account is not authorized to access this application. Please contact the administrator.
login.accessRestricted: Access is restricted to authorized GitHub accounts.

dashboard.noCaptures: No captures yet. Upload a .pcap file above.

admin.failedToLoad: Failed to load allowed users. Please try again.
admin.noUsers: No users configured. Add a GitHub username above.

uploader.dropHere: Drop a <accent>.pcap</accent> or <accent>.pcapng</accent> file here, or click to browse

packetTree.selectPacket: Select a packet to view protocol details

hexViewer.selectPacket: Select a packet to view hex dump

captureCommand.openCaptureToChat: Open a capture to use chat.
captureCommand.describeCapture: Describe what you want to capture
captureCommand.tcpdumpPlaceholder: e.g. "Capture all HTTPS traffic to example.com on eth0"

followStream.title: Follow Stream
followStream.noPayload: No payload data in this conversation.
followStream.truncated: Stream truncated at the size limit.

tcpdump.iface: Network interface to capture packets on. Use 'any' to capture on all interfaces.
tcpdump.count: Stop after capturing this many packets. Omit for unlimited capture.
tcpdump.snapLen: Bytes to capture per packet. 0 means full packet. Default varies by version (often 262144).
tcpdump.bufferSize: Kernel buffer size in KiB. Increase for high-traffic captures to avoid drops.
tcpdump.writeFile: Write raw packets to a file instead of printing to stdout. Creates a pcap file.
tcpdump.readFile: Read packets from a previously saved capture file instead of live capture.
tcpdump.verbose: Increase output verbosity. Each level adds more protocol detail to the output.
tcpdump.noDns: Control DNS resolution. -n skips host lookup, -nn also skips port name lookup.
tcpdump.hexMode: Print each packet as hex dump. -X includes ASCII, -XX adds link-level header.
tcpdump.timestamp: Control timestamp format. Options range from no timestamp to high-resolution delta times.
tcpdump.showAscii: Print each packet in ASCII. Useful for capturing text-based protocols like HTTP.
tcpdump.lineBuffered: Use line-buffered output. Useful when piping tcpdump to another program like grep.
tcpdump.protocol: Filter by protocol. Only packets matching this protocol will be captured.
tcpdump.hostFilter: Filter by host IP address or hostname. Combine with direction (src/dst) for one-way filtering.
tcpdump.hostDirection: Direction qualifier for host filter: match source, destination, or both.
tcpdump.port: Filter by port number. Use 'portrange' direction for a range like 80-443.
tcpdump.portDirection: Direction qualifier for port filter: match source port, destination port, or both.
tcpdump.net: Filter by network using CIDR notation. Captures all traffic to/from that subnet.
tcpdump.netDirection: Direction qualifier for network filter: match source, destination, or both.
tcpdump.customBpf: Raw BPF (Berkeley Packet Filter) expression. Combined with other filters using 'and'.

pktmon.compId: Select networking components to monitor by ID. Use 'pktmon list' to see available components.
pktmon.packetSize: Maximum bytes to log per packet. Omit to log full packet content.
pktmon.fileName: Output ETL file name. Default is PktMon.etl in the current directory.
pktmon.fileSize: Maximum log file size in megabytes. The capture stops when this limit is reached.
pktmon.logMode: Logging mode: 'circular' overwrites oldest data; 'multi-file' creates new files at size limit.
pktmon.packetType: Filter by packet type: 'flow' for normal traffic, 'drop' for dropped packets only.
pktmon.transport: Filter by transport protocol. Only matching packets are captured.
pktmon.ipAddress: Filter by IP address. Only packets with this source or destination IP are captured.
pktmon.port: Filter by port number. Only packets on this port are captured.
pktmon.countersOnly: Log packet counters only, not packet contents. Lightweight monitoring mode.
pktmon.dropReasons: Include the reason why each packet was dropped by the networking stack.
pktmon.convertToPcapng: Append a command to convert the ETL output to pcapng format for use in Wireshark.
